record.field: Postiz is an open-source social media management platform that schedules, automates, and measures content across dozens of channels from a single self-hosted dashboard.

Postiz is an open-source social media management platform that schedules, automates, and measures content across dozens of channels from a single self-hosted dashboard.

It acts as a self-hosted alternative to tools like Buffer, Hypefury, and Later, letting individuals, agencies, and teams plan and publish posts to 20+ networks (X, Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube, Reddit, Threads, Mastodon, Bluesky, Discord, Pinterest and more). Built on a TypeScript stack (Next.js front end, NestJS back end, PostgreSQL and Redis), it authenticates directly against each platform's official OAuth flow rather than storing third-party API keys. AI-assisted composition, team collaboration, and an automation API round it out for hands-off content operations.

Key features
  • Schedule and publish posts to 20+ social networks from one calendar
  • AI-assisted content generation, rewriting, and per-channel adaptation
  • Analytics and measurement to track reach and engagement
  • Team collaboration with comments, approvals, and multiple members
  • Automation API and integrations with n8n, Make, Zapier, and MCP agents
  • Direct per-platform OAuth authentication, no stored third-party API keys
Best for

Creators, marketing teams, and agencies that want a privacy-respecting, self-hosted hub to plan, automate, and analyze social media publishing across many channels without relying on a commercial SaaS.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_automation'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_ai'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_collaboration'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_analytics'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_workflow'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_marketing')])]

record.field: services: postiz-app: image: ghcr.io/gitroomhq/postiz-app:latest restart: always environment: MAIN_URL: https://{{ postiz_host }} FRONTEND_URL: https://{{ postiz_host }} NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL: https://{{ postiz_host }}/api JWT_SECRET: {{ jwt_secret }} DATABASE_URL: postgresql://{{ db_user }}:{{ db_password }}@postiz-postgres:5432/{{ db_name }} REDIS_URL: redis://postiz-redis:6379 BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL: http://localhost:3000 IS_GENERAL: 'true' STORAGE_PROVIDER: local UPLOAD_DIRECTORY: /uploads NEXT_PUBLIC_UPLOAD_DIRECTORY: /uploads volumes: - ./postiz_config:/config/ - ./postiz_uploads:/uploads/ depends_on: postiz-postgres: condition: service_healthy postiz-redis: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:5000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network postiz-postgres: image: postgres:17-alpine restart: always environment: POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ db_password }} POSTGRES_USER: {{ db_user }} POSTGRES_DB: {{ db_name }} volumes: - ./postiz_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: pg_isready -U {{ db_user }} -d {{ db_name }} interval: 10s timeout: 3s retries: 3 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postiz_postgres' networks: - internal postiz-redis: image: redis:7.2 restart: always healthcheck: test: redis-cli ping interval: 10s timeout: 3s retries: 3 volumes: - ./postiz_redis_data:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postiz_redis' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postiz-postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postiz-postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postiz-postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postiz-postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postiz-postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
